The incident occurred when the 10-year-old girl, whose name has not been released, was prescribed the antibiotic by her doctor to treat a bacterial infection. Within minutes of taking the medication, the girl began experiencing symptoms of an allergic reaction, including hives, difficulty breathing, and swelling of the face and throat.

Her parents immediately rushed her to the hospital, where doctors determined that she was suffering from anaphylaxis, a severe and potentially life-threatening allergic reaction. Despite their best efforts to stabilize her condition, the girl's body continued to react to the penicillin, causing her skin to blister and peel, as if she was being burnt from the inside out.

The medical team is currently working around the clock to treat the girl's condition and minimize the damage caused by the allergic reaction. However, the road to recovery is expected to be long and difficult.

This tragic incident serves as a reminder of the importance of being aware of potential allergies and sensitivities to medications. It is crucial for both patients and healthcare providers to communicate openly about any known allergies in order to prevent such devastating reactions from occurring in the future.
